entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi @alex_bundschuh Thanks for the Pipeline concept, It is really great approach and more helpful too.

In my case, I have Multiple IDocs coming from single port and single partner profile at the movement. planning to have a single destination with single port and using the generic IFlow, I have to route the individual IDocs into individual receivers Iflows, where I need to do the mappings or transformation logics as per the individual receivers expectations and when I talk about the receivers, we have multiple receiver applications but we need to route the multiple IDocs to the same receiver using their own APIs and as per the mapping expectations.

as per my above scenario, I am trying to implement the pipeline steps, but getting error in pipeline step 4(Pipeline Generic Step04 - Receiver Determination) error with below screenshot

can I get the step by step configuration document to setup/ implement the Pipeline steps for my scenarios please?

entry.title: Re: RAP - Root page action button, with single selection...
Re: Rounding Discrepancy for Goal Results Payout Amount –...
entry.content: Hi @xavierlegarrec, thank you very much for your feedback. You are correct, KBA is referring to different field. I was linking this KBA because the issue described was similar, but we experienced the issue with the field "Goal Results Payout Amount". We were able to resolve the issue by increasing the decimal places for all money fields, not only the "Goal Results Payout Amount" field. Best regards, Ann-Kathrin

entry.title: Announcing the Learning Journey: Developing with SAP Customer Data Platform
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of our brand-new learning journey course: Developing with SAP Customer Data Platform. This comprehensive learning journey is now available and designed to empower developers with the skills needed to leverage the full potential of the SAP Customer Data Platform.

 

Upon completion of this course, you will be able to successfully create and manage administrative credentials for accessing the SAP Customer Data Platform API, construct code to utilize the Query and Ingestion APIs, perform Event Metadata configuration, set up an Event Listener, and program and configure Events to enrich Customer Data.

By the end of this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Create and manage administrative credentials required for accessing the SAP Customer Data Platform API
  • Construct code to utilize the Query API to effectively search and retrieve customer data
  • Develop code to use the Ingestion API to add and update customer data within the CDP
  • Perform Event Metadata configuration using the deduplication ID and partition event data with the timestamp field
  • Set up an Event Listener to ingest customer data
  • Program and configure events to enrich customer data using extensions

 

Before starting this e-learning, ensure you familiarize yourself with the SAP Customer Data Platform by completing our essential e-learning course: Exploring SAP Customer Data Platform.

Be sure to visit our learning site, where you can explore a wide array of videos about the  SAP Customer Data Platform.

We hope you enjoy this learning journey and encourage you to share your valuable feedback with us. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Special Gl indicator in billing Document
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, Based on your reply, request further guidance, in VKOA, we assign only GL accounts against account key. If we want the customer a/c to hit a sp gl recon a/c, then how can that be achived via VKOA. Would you be able to share any screenshot to advise, thanks

entry.title: Re: Clarifications Regarding SAP Disclosure Management Mi...
entry.content:
Hi,

The IAAS servers comes as you rightfully state in sizes 32/64/128GiB memory. We have a Linux and a Windows version. The default allocated storage is for operational purpose only, so any storage needed have to be added with the "SAP Additional File Storage" service. This is sold in 128GB blocks.

The partner/customer will get full OS Admin rights.
Partner/customer is fully responsible for operational services, including installation and backup. SAP will remove our own access once this is delivered, so it becomes a blackbox for us. SAP can restart the server if needed.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Adding Material Basic Data Text field to the Goods Receipt for PO collective slip PDF Output
entry.content:
Hello everyone!!! First I would like to report that I am a newbie and a newcomer to this community.

As per the subject I wanted to know if there was a way to add the Basic Data Text to the Goods Receipt for PO collective slip PDF Output. I have already created a custom field and extended the form template data source with this. Now I don't know how to enhance this custom field with the Basic Data Text.

I also found the extension point MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA which also seems to recognize my custom field; but I have no idea how to get my hands on the code.

Would anyone be able to help me?

Thank You 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Launchpad: New Fast and Easy Option to Adapt SAP-delivered Launchpad Content (for On
entry.content:
Hi Manasa,

'If we do the adaptation by the process you suggested that to copy to custom catalog - Do all the SAP upgrades also applied automatically to the adapted apps.'

Yes, you also have an advantage during future upgrades: SAP’s updates to the SAP technical catalog will automatically show up on the launchpad of your business users. Except for the fields that you have adapted, of course – after all, you would not want your adapted tile title to disappear after an upgrade.

Best,
Ruth

entry.title: Manager Form Eligibility Rules - Variable Pay Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

we are currently implementing a non-EC integrated VP template. 

Customer requested Manager Form Eligibility rules based on Hire Date. 

However, this information is only available in EP and cannot be included in the UDF (custom0-15 fields are already blocked). 

When checking the Manager Form Eligibility rule settings, I can see the following option: 

Does anyone of you know why Hire Date is provided as a default selection?

Does this mean that VP can read the Hire Date from EP, or would the Hire Date still need to be part of the VP form for the rule to work? 

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you very much in advance and best regards

Ann-Kathrin

entry.title: Re: My app can not export PDF anymore after Windows 11 up...
entry.content: I have not tried ".NET and CR for VS SP37" yet. I think it is maybe the most realistic solution. In SAP Crystal Report 2016 there is a ActiveXControls catalogue with CRViewer.dll etc. I wonder if there some examples and how to use this one in a VB6 program AND are I allowed to do so by the license?

entry.title: How to inspect several material batch produced on one Production order ?
entry.content:
Hi dear colleagues.

I have to inspect material batches in production (03 origin, or 04, perhaps both).

When my Production Order produces several material batches, I wanted to avoïd to create one inspection lot for each material batch. My favourite idea is "inspection point", with a meaning to create one inspection point for each material batch created from my Production Order.

What do you think of that ?

Someone already did that ? Or other way ?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: While Booking Invoice QR IBAN - Validation with Vendor master data
entry.content: Hi Kumar, at the end did you find any solution? What was the workaround in OB32? what did you change? Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAP Business ByDesign: Issue with PO Reference Overriding
entry.content:
Scenario:
We have a customer (Customer AB) with four Purchase Orders (POs) across two years:

2024:

Document A (Aug 2024) – PO Ref: 1234
Document B (Nov 2024) – PO Ref: 1234
Document C (Dec 2024) – PO Ref: 1234
2025:

Document D (Jan 2025) – PO Ref: 5678 (PO reference changed)
Issue:
When generating an Excel report from SAP, all four documents (A, B, C, and D) now display the new PO reference 5678, even though documents A, B, and C were originally associated with PO reference 1234.

While the invoices remain unchanged, the end user wants documents A, B, and C to retain PO Ref 1234, while only document D should reflect 5678.

Query:

  • Is there a standard way in #SAP Business ByDesign to prevent PO reference updates from overriding historical records?
  • Are there any workarounds or settings in the report generation process that could help retain the correct PO references per document?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Adding Long Term Backup to existing Contract - What a...
entry.content:
Hi,

We cannot provide backup retention beyond the contract end date.

You can go for the 4-year retention, knowing this will effectively end on contract end date and only if customer choose to extend this will continue for the remaining period.

Normally a retention period is determined by legal or business requirements and this should be the determining factor of which to choose.

BR Henrik

entry.title: While doing EBRS in S4Hana public cloud Check in and Check out GL balance not getting cleared
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While doing EBRS in S4Hana public cloud Check in and Check out GL balance not getting cleared. Main bank GL account balance is getting increasing or decreasing based on the transaction but clearing account transactions (check in and check out GL) still showing as open item. 

Suppose i am doing check in bank GL reconciliation with MT940 format following posting will happen

1. Main (11001000)GL Dr and Cash receipt (11001080) GL Cr

2. Cash receipt (11001080) GL Dr and Main (11001000)GL Cr

thus making no difference in both GL balance.

Can anyone help me how to clear check in /Check out GL balance by using EBRS.

Regards,

Nitin

entry.title: Re: Delivery should not be allowed to create if there is no stock

entry.title: Re: Delivery should not be allowed to create if there is no stock

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Launchpad: Dead process after 2 hours
entry.content: Hi, were you able to fix this one? I'm having the same issues and I'm not sure how to proceed. I Updated my Docker secret multiple times, I'm sure it is correct. I'm following this troubleshooting - https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/docker

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
@Heidilee 

Hi,

There are a lot of limitations that we have observed while testing in our development enviourmnet, while some of them will be resolved in the coming update of 1H 2025 for the other limitations there is no update on when they will have a similar feature in the New Learning Homepage. The following are the limitations we found that should have something similar in New learning Homepage :

  • The "Launch Proxy" action (Learning Administration > People > Users > Actions) will display the legacy Learning home page, even with the "New Learning Experience" toggle on for the user being proxied.
  • Blank searching or browsing in Enhanced Search is not available. Minimum 2 characters are needed for a search.
  • New Items, curricula and programs and changes to existing items, curricula and programs will take up to 24 hours to be available in the production system and in the stage environments, items added to a library may take up to a week to be visible to users. 
  • Calendar View is not available in the Find Learning page.
  • All sections such as Required Learning, Important Learning, etc. will only show up to 8 cards. The View All option must be used to see more.
  • Optional Surveys and Follow-Up surveys won’t be available. (Surveys which are required for item completion will show on the new home page. Only Course Feedback surveys impact item completion.)
  • The item relaunch warning that appears when the item was completed in the past is currently not supported.
  • In the bookmarks, sections will reappear even after dismissed when the page is refreshed/ users navigate away and back to homepage/ New Learning Experience toggle is turned off and on again.
  • Optional items that are part of curriculum won't be displayed on the new learning page.
  • Highlight as "Featured", "New" and "Revised" items won’t be displayed in the items as there's a flag filter in Enhanced Search page to filter by the same.
  • Setting Priority is not supported.
  • When email notifications are disabled and the "Send Reminder" button available in the Team View area is clicked, emails are generated and queued. If the queued emails aren't purged and the emails are enabled, they're sent.
  • Thumbnail images not appearing for Required Learning and Important Learning section

Could anybody let me know if there is any timeline for migrating these features from Legacy to NLH?

entry.title: CNMASSCREATE
entry.content: I am working with CNMASSCREATE. I have been able to create the project definition without any problem. I have had problems creating the TOP, but I have been able to create it. But I cannot create the lower level WBS. The curious thing is that when I execute the transaction, it shows me warnings due to differences between project calendars that are different from the factory calendar of the center, bur no errors. But in CJ20N, these lower level WBS do not appear to be created. Does anyone know why this happens?

entry.title: Error while running CDS view from datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

I created the following CDS view.

@EndUserText.label: 'CDS della tabella AGR_DEFINE'

@AbapCatalog: { sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_CFO_AGRDEF',
compiler.compareFilter: true,
preserveKey: true
}

@VDM.viewType: #BASIC
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@analytics: {
dataCategory: #CUBE,
internalName: #LOCAL,
dataExtraction: {
enabled: true,
delta.changeDataCapture: {
automatic: true
}
}
}

define view ZCDS_AGR_DEFINE
as select from agr_define
{
key agr_name,
parent_agr,
create_usr,
create_dat,
create_tim,
create_tmp,
change_usr,
change_dat,
change_tim,
change_tmp,
attributes
}

Importing it into Datasphere, and running the full extraction it gives me the following error

The source connector has accepted the initial load partitioning request. The next attempt will be after 05-Feb-2025 11:03:32 UTC. CorrelationId: 944e71c5-2c9b-4634-1900-bc0205bd33f0@05-Feb-2025 11:03:28.

How can I solve the problem?

entry.title: Re: Make your Legacy SAP WM a Stock Room Management Compliant for WCU Interfaces
entry.content:
Hi Prakash, 

Just curious on how to deal with inbound WCU IDoc types WMTORD and WMTOCO? Need new Process codes and Function modules to process the custom Idoc types ZWMTORD and ZWMTOCO?

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @dyaryura ,

thank you for your feedback. SCIM is definitely an important standard on both sides, and SAP and Microsoft support it across many components as seen in this scenario.

Regarding your question on the concept of "source system" and enrichment of attributes: In this scenario, Entra is the "leading" identity directory. Identites are created/updated/deleted here (either directly in the Entra ID tenant, through synchronization from on-prem, or via inbound provisioning from an external HR system, such as SF). This scenario also takes the architectual decision to centralize any attribute transformation logic in the Entra tenant. As an example, the user's SAP login name and SNC mapping are dynamically constructed with the mapping expressions in steps 6.17 to 6.19, and passed-through "as-is" by the IPS transformations. One could also decide to apply such transformation logic in IPS, but I'd always recommend to do this at a central place.

In one of the following parts of this series, I plan to take a closer look at the integration with SF. Again, Entra will stay in the role of the leading identity directory, and will therefore be configured for inbound provisioning from SF, and from there provision to CIS. We will also look at more complex attribute enrichment/transformation scenarios through custom extensions in Entra. Please stay tuned 😉

Following the reference architecure from SAP, it remains the responsibility of CIS/IPS to orchestrate provisioning to multiple ABAP systems and to SAP SaaS solutions.

Integration with IAG is also on our joint roadmap, and will play a central role in the provisioning process, e.g. to check for SOD violations before assiging a user to an access package in Entra ID Governance.

Hope that helps to answer (some of) your questions.

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: Created Quantity Contract. But while creating order with reference to the Contract, it also accepts
entry.content: We have created  Contracts (WK1 and WK2 both ). While creating the order with reference to the Contracts, it is also accepting the materials not included in the contract. Where is the issue? Also if we make the order value above the Target Value of contract, then also its accepting and order is getting saved also. Since I am a beginner in SAP but as per my understanding of the Contract topic,both the scenarios should give error. Any help or Guidance in this regard will be highly appreciable.

entry.title: Posting Journal Entries with alternative accounts in SAP Public Cloud / S4 via API
entry.content:
Hello,

I've setup a correct JE entry transaction sending from an external system to a customer SAP S4 Cloud Public Edition, and I'm using this standard SAP API to integrate JE transactions into SAP via XML data:

Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous)

https://api.sap.com/api/JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUESTCONFI/overview

However, since customer uses an alternative chart of accounts in SAP due to country financial requirements, it requested to change the integration to use alternative accounts instead of internal SAP accounts.

Is there any way to send these alternative accounts in the Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous) XML message?

Currently I'm using the XML tag <GLAccount> to send the internal SAP JE accounts but could find any tag to send alternative accounts...

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Distribution of Packing Instructions as Packaging Specifications in EWM
entry.content:
hi,

Not having option to CIF for specific Production unit is a big business gap..

CFM1 - filter Pack instructions based on criteria

CFM2 - finds condition records where pack instructions is used and distribute it, one pack instructions can be used by different plants by creating condition records.

Technical gap  - only one queue gets created on EWM side so if there are thousands of records under one queue and 1 records get failed whole queue get stuck.

Screenshots of the steps followed to upload the File:

Target file - Phyinv34_38

Ankit

entry.title: Re: Outbound Internet Traffic Tunneling From RISE to On-p...
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

The default gateway is the customers entry point into the private cloud, so unless customer request this to be changed, all traffic will reach the customers firewall.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Workday to Ariba integration for user data

entry.title: Re: Inventory count entry by Batch-Input Method with Transaction MI34, MI38, MI39, MI40
CCM - Statistical price condition in delta accruals
entry.content:
Dear Guru,

We are implementing CCM in SAP S/4HANA and have encountered an error while posting delta accruals. In our scenario, we need to create a settlement document for delta accruals with a net amount of 0, where the accounting posting conditions are defined as "Statistical."

To achieve this, we have been conducting tests using the standard document (settlement document 0S42: Sales Rebate Delta Accruals and pricing schema A10006: Rebates Delta Accrual (Germany)) with the following configurations:

  • 0S42 – Section: "Accounting"
    • "Posting statistical condition to accounting" set to "YES (without cash discount)"
    • "Customer posting rules" set to "0A Accruals only"
  • 0S42 – Section: "Control of Account Determination"
    • "Account key source for material adjustment" set to "From Pricing Procedure: Accruals Account Key"
  • Schema A10006
  • RED1 set as "Statistical" and "Relevant for account determination"

When we created the delta accrual document, we encountered several issues:

  • RED1 does not have the "Accrual" flag, although RED1 refers to REA1 according to the standard guide (REA1 is flagged for "Accruals").
  • The account key for accruals is not triggered – instead, the system selects the account key for G/L.
  • When we attempt to release the FI document, the system indicates success. However, when clicking on "Accounting," no document is displayed. There are no dumps, error logs, or incorrect updates in the database.

Do you suggest any alternative approaches to resolve this issue?

Thank you

Michele

entry.title: Receiving 997 functional acknowledgement in SAP with TPM in CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts
From B2B partner we are receiving 997 functional acknowledgement to CPI , but we dont find any option to route this through mapping to the ECC system. Any inputs will be highly appreciated.Thanks

entry.title: SAP BTP Accessing Feature Flag using ClientCredentials Destination. Part - 1
entry.content:
Introduction to SAP Feature Flags

The SAP Feature Flags service is designed to help developers manage feature toggles efficiently. By using feature flags, you can control the availability of new features in your application dynamically. This blog post will walk you through a simple application that accesses a feature flag from the Feature Flag dashboard and uses its return value to control application behavior based on different requirements.

Not going to describe more, you can find the more details in sap documentation - link

Pre-Requisites

Before diving into the implementation, ensure you have the following:

  • SAP BTP Feature Flag subscription
  • Feature Flag Instance
  • Flag created in the Feature Flag Dashboard
  • Destination instance

For this demo, we have set up the SAP BTP Feature Flag service, created an instance of the feature flag service, and a destination named featureflagdest.

Setting Up the Environment

Creating the Certificate

First, create the necessary certificates for OAuth2ClientCredentials authentication.

Use the following commands in the BTP command prompt:

 

cf service-key feature-flag flag-key | sed '/Getting key/d' | jq --raw-output .credentials.x509.certificate > mtls-certificate.pem
cf service-key feature-flag flag-key | sed '/Getting key/d' | jq --raw-output .credentials.x509.key > mtls-private-key.pem

 

Both of these commands will extract certificate details and store in .pem files.

Combine the contents of mtls-certificate.pem and mtls-private-key.pem into a single certificate.pem file and upload it to the certificates section inside the destination service featureflagdest.

Creating the Destination

Create a destination named FeatureDest with the following details:

  • Name: FeatureDest
  • Type: HTTP
  • URL: https://feature-flags.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com
  • Proxy Type: Internet
  • Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • Use mTLS for token retrieval: Checked
  • Client ID:
  • Token Service Key Store Location: certificate.pem
  • Token Service Key Store Password: Auto-generated from certificate.pem
  • Token Service URL Type: Dedicated
  • Token Service URL: https://trial-<subdomain>.authentication.cert.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Most of the inputs we will get from the flag-key credentials –

 

We are done creating our destination. We could use Basic Authentication method but thought to use certificate-based ClientCredentials authentication and how to create the .pem file out of the service key.

In the next part we will develop a small application to access the Feature Flag.

--> SAP BTP Accessing Feature Flag using ClientCredentials Destination. Part - 2

entry.title: SAP Crystal Server 2020 TRIAL - Is Business View Manager available
entry.content:
Good morning,
     I’m testing trial version of Crystal Server 2020. I got license and key valid for 60 days.
After I installed the platform I can’t find Business Views Manager (no icon, no group in Start -> Programs etc.).

Is it proper behaviour?  Shall it be like that  for trial?

I have compared folders of Crystal Server 2008 and 2020 and I can’t find binary related with Business View Manager (mentioned below).
It is simply not available as it was in old Crystal Server 2008 in similar path:
"X:\Program Files\Business Objects\BusinessObjects Enterprise 12.0\win32_x86\CrystalBVM.exe"

I have checked and I can see this in my fresh installed SAP Crystal 2020 Server software:
"X:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win32_x86"
"X:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64" but there is no binary like CrystalBVM.exe in it.
I can provide more details if needed.

Thanks for your help in advance.

entry.title: Re: AATP oprions for manual allocation/re-allocation
entry.content:
Dear Dequan Xu,

Thanks so much for your answer regarding BOP and PAL functionalities.

The issue we are facing in the distribution is not that simple and need really fast reaction from our side.  My concern is that we have around 200 customers and majority of them collecting their goods once a week. 90% of our customers are in the same priority level and can happen that this week customer A asking some xtra qty to avoid line stop on the other week customer B and C asking the same. On top of this these are common items using both customer A,B and C. Also we obliged to supply them the requested quantity on the LT so in case we are helping one customer,  can happen that we cannot keep our contractual obligations towards the other customers. 

I know well that safety stock keeping is one option here but learnt that in case the safety is set,  handling of allocation is also depending on AATP so there is no manual intervention possible... Our original idea was to keep some safety and not allow ATP to calculate with , but we will allocate form this safety manually during delivery creation to that customers who have troubles. However turned out that this is not possible since we cannot put more to the delivery than ATP confirmed previously so not possible to allocate more manually from the safety stock.

I know it is not an easy business but we are still looking for a solution that in case our confirmation is not acceptable to our customer(s) we somehow allocate or re-allocate the available stock between them. PAL is a solution, yes for a limited number of parts but unfortunately we are talking about 1-2thousands of parts with huge fluctuations from one week to the other. I also understand the features of BOP, however changing priority on a daily or weekly basis is not optimal at all.

We are well befor the go live but this is a very unconfortable situation that such basic options are missing or cannot find optimal solution for it.

entry.title: Re: OUT NOW: Standard integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: When will it be possible to sync Signavio Processes to existing Cloud ALM processes? The consulting solution solved this by adding an attribute in Signavio with the ID of the CALM process. The roadmap is quite empty so far 😮

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Sydney: Share, Connect and Inspire! - Friday 21 March 2025
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Sydney is starting the year strong with the first event for 2025.  Come and join us to learn about:

  • Enterprise Architecture and SAP BTP: Better Together
  • Lions journey from on prem to SAAS via RISE. Long term vision to enable business agility and reduce TCO
  • Introduction to DevOps
  • Business Use Cases for AI

For more information and to register, please use only this Eventbrite link.
Responding on the Will you be attending? panel does not qualify as a registration.

entry.title: Rise in Hypersaler IBM
entry.content:
SAP.

Is it possible today to provision client systems in the RISE model in the IBM Datacenter?

Note 2414820 - SAP in the IBM Cloud: support prerequisites, details support for this infrastructure.

entry.title: Re: Accelerate your period-end journal entries with AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases
entry.content: Thanks, @saurabh_saxena for the very informative blog!

entry.title: Re: How we started with ABAP OO
entry.content:
Agree on "end-of-selection" not being used now that it has been declared obsolete.  It is not necessary w/o an LDB.  I do a lot of HR/ABAP and for reports (at least for my own) I know immediately if an LDB is in play when I see "end-of-selection".

However, when the OP set up their class "end-of-selection" was used commonly w/o LDBs throughout the ABAP world and  old programs must be maintained, too.

I know that LDBs are also said to be obsolete by SAP.  Good luck with that going forward since there are thousands (millions likely) of customer code using SAP/HR LDBs and even SAP uses them in their on premises SAP/HANA as far as I know.

Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is a concept.
"Premises" is a location.

"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: under clarification by Export Control
entry.content:
How to resolve this problem ?

Your request is under clarification by Export Control. We will send you an email as soon as the clarification has been provided.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: replicate sales orders from ecc to crm
entry.content:
I know this is an old thread. Also this last link does not appear to be working any more.

My issue is a bit opposite. I have sales orders replicating from ECC to CRM. But I wanted to turn it off. I went to R3AC1, and under SALESDOCUMENT I removed all the OLTP filters, assuming that it will stop replicating the orders to CRM. But I am clearly seeing more BUS_TRANS_MSG in the system with the Sender Site OLTP.

I thought these filters control what gets replicated over to CRM. Something else is missing?

entry.title: SAP BTP Accessing Feature Flag using ClientCredentials Destination. Part - 2
entry.content:
In Previous Part-1 we configured the destination through which we will access feature flags. Now we will develop an application. We could use a Simple NodeJs application to access BTP instance, but again thought of developing using sap cloud sdk as described by SAP.

How to develop a simple nodeJS application, you can follow one of my existing blog posts or any other out there in internet.

Run NodeJs in SAP BTP and Locally, Part - 1

(** Note: The blog was written earlier, you may have to adjust the npm packages and little modifications in the code.)

 Let’s Start with some command to install packages –

Setting Up the Project

1. Install NestJS: (documentation on NestJS - link )

 

 

npm i -g @nestjs/cli

 

 

2. Scaffold an Application:

 

 

nest new featureflagsapcloudsdk
cd featureflagsapcloudsdk

 

 

3. Install OpenAPI Generator:

 

 

npm install -D -cloud-sdk/openapi-generator

 

 

4. Download and Set Up FeatureFlagsAPI.yaml:

    • Download from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
    • Create folders resources/service-specs and upload FeatureFlagsAPI.yaml. https://api.sap.com/api/FeatureFlagsAPI/overview

                       

5. Create a file options-per-service.json and store in the same folder ‘resources/service-specs’.

 

 

{
  "resources/service-specs/FeatureFlagsAPI.yaml": {}
}

 

 

6. Install SAP Cloud SDK OpenAPI Package:

 

 

npm i -cloud-sdk/openapi

 

 

7. Generate Typed Client:

 

 

npx openapi-generator --input resources/service-specs --outputDir src/generated

 

 

This will generate the below like folders –

                               

All the calls or routing will be navigating from app.controller.ts file. These files will be generated by-default.

                           

8. Implementing the Application

Modify app.controller.ts and app.service.ts to include methods for accessing the feature flag.

These applications are based on typescript which generated by sap cloud sdk, but for the demo purposes we are avoiding the types. Mostly we are providing <any> as assigning and return type.

app.controller.ts:

app.service.ts:

Add “import { EvaluateV2Api } from './generated/FeatureFlagsAPI';”

9. Deploying the Application

Now let’s create a manifest file to deploy and bind with other resources –

Destination Featureflagdest already

** I am not very much worried about the authentication here now. So only using the Destination service.

manifest.yaml will look like below –

Before deploying use the below Command, which is must each time you make any changes to any files.

 

 

nest build

 

 

Now deploy manifest file to BTP Cloud foundry using Command

 

 

cf push

 

 

10. Run the Application

In BTP, Application is in running state and started.

             

Execute the module with the path /feature-flag

Here is the output when flag is set to true

When the flag is false

Code Snippet –

app.controller.ts

 

 

import { Controller, Get, HttpException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppService } from './app.service';
@Controller()
export class AppController {
  constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) { }
  @Get('/feature-flag')
  getFeatureFlag(): any {    
    return this.appService.getFlag()
    .then(response => {
      return response;
    })
    .catch(error => {
      throw new HttpException(
        `Failed to get Feature Flags - ${error.message}`,
        500
      )
    })
  }
}

 

 

app.service.ts

 

 

import { Injectable, Get ,HttpException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { EvaluateV2Api } from './generated/FeatureFlagsAPI';
@Injectable()
export class AppService {
  async getFlag(): Promise<any> {
    return EvaluateV2Api
          .getV2EvaluateById('<feature_flag_name>', { identifier: '<tenant_id>'})
          .setBasePath('https://feature-flags.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/api')
          .execute({ destinationName: '<destination_name>' });
  }
}

 

 

manifest.yaml

 

 

applications:
  - name: featureflagsdk  
    path: .
    buildpacks:
      - nodejs_buildpack
    memory: 256M
    command: npm run start:prod
    random-route: true
    services:
      - featureflagdest

 

 

 

 

References –

  1. https://help.sap.com/docs/feature-flags-service/sap-feature-flags-service/what-is-sap-feature-flags-service
  2. https://api.sap.com/api/FeatureFlagsAPI/overview
  3. https://sap.github.io/cloud-sdk/

entry.title: How to get the total number of data point comments in the model for Analytic Model?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am looking to get the total count of data point comments from the model which is present as a separate column in the report table. Is there any way to check the number of comments made?

Model is analytical one with data point comments enabled

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: Fully Autonomous Finance? Is that our Goal? (A Case for Human-in-the-Middle Scenarios)
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving world of finance, AI is transforming how businesses operate. From automating routine tasks like transaction matching to summarizing and analyzing complex data sets including structured and non-structured information, AI offers remarkable efficiencies.

However, the human element remains crucial in the AI process, ensuring that technology serves its intended purpose effectively and ethically, while meeting specific compliance requirements.

Firstly, human oversight is essential for maintaining ethical standards. AI systems can process vast amounts of data, but they lack the ability to understand the nuances of ethical decision-making. Humans provide the moral compass, ensuring that AI applications align with societal values and regulatory requirements. This oversight helps prevent biases and ensures fairness.

SAP’s approach to Business AI addresses the requirements of ethical responsibility in AI – as well as relevance and reliability in AI. SAP has an AI ethics steering committee and an AI ethics advisory panel with industry leaders to ensure the ethical use of AI in the business context. These teams work with our customers for a more ethical use of AI that aligns with our core values.

Secondly, human expertise adds a layer of critical thinking that AI cannot replicate. While AI excels at identifying patterns and trends, it doesn't possess the intuition and judgment that come from years of experience. Financial professionals can interpret AI-generated insights, considering broader economic contexts and existing relationships with business partners and networks. This leads to more informed decisionmaking considering factors that machines alone might miss. It’s then the combination of the human and AI that delivers more value that leads to better decisionmaking.

Example:  Finance experts provide detailed commentary on financial results. AI can generate a summary that combines the commentary with financial analysis, making a well-explained, comprehensive view of business performance available to business managers that may not be financial experts.

In the world of finance, there could be a legal burden of proof when decisions lead to results that destroy shareholder value. For example, in Germany, this applies to both the management boards of publicly traded stock corporations (AG) as well as limited liability companies (GmbH), regardless of their size. In these cases, company officers might be required to show the data they used as a basis for their decisions[1]. This often includes demonstrating how the risks and opportunities were weighed, and that the decisions that were made can be reasonably considered to be “in the best interest of the organization”. If AI is making decisions autonomously, it might be impossible to re-create the data foundation of the decision. This could leave company officers liable to legal consequences.

Moreover, humans play a vital role in balancing risk and rewards, based on professional experience. AI systems, though powerful, are not infallible. They can make errors or be vulnerable to cyber threats. Human involvement ensures continuous monitoring and quick intervention when anomalies occur, safeguarding financial systems from potential disruptions.

AI at SAP: Human-in-the-Middle

At SAP, as AI is embedded into finance business processes, the processes are designed to keep experts involved. In the early days of AI, machine learning provided recommendations for expert intervention based on previous experience – for example suggesting a write-off of a particilar goods-and-invoice-receipt reconciliation issue. The system proposed the “(most-likely) next step” option for moving the process forward. The accountant would then restart the process with a single click.

With the evolution of generative AI, SAP Business AI can propose the mapping of one chart of accounts to a standard chart, suggest configuration settings for confirmation, and even explain the accounting details behind the evolution of asset values over times.

The current focus of many teams currently are AI agents. AI agents act as co-workers for accountants, assisting in real time. The accountant has the experience of having their own dedicated, super-personalized assistant that knows all your data, speeds up tasks, and enhances decision-making.

AI agents handle the most simple processing tasks and summarize decision options for the accountant. In these cases, the accountant is making the key decisions, and the follow-on actions are triggered via workflows that are performed by a combination of real and virtual agents.

In these examples you can see that AI supports and assists the accountant for more effective outcomes. While AI prepares analyses for decisionmaking, it’s the human that makes the decision.

Example: AI can use your company’s specific accounting policy and your sales forecast data to derive accrual postings related to sales commissions. However, rather than post these accruals directly, the system generates a posting proposal which is confirmed by the local accountant and then approved by the accounting manager. The journal posting is triggered only after this principle of dual control is followed. This reduces compliance risk for the CFO who remains personally responsible for the completeness and accuracy of corporate financial reports in many jurisdications. Read this blog for more details.

In conclusion, while AI revolutionizes finance, the human touch remains indispensable. By integrating human insight and control, businesses can harness AI's full potential, ensuring a future where technology and accountants work hand in hand. Accountants are not going to be replaced by AI. Accountants who use AI effectively will replace accountants who don’t.

 

Learn more about SAP Business AI in Finance here: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/finance.html

 

(And thanks to my colleague Daniel Welzbacher for his input!)

[1] Business Judgement Rule (§93(1)(2) Germany’s Stock Corporation Act (AktG)).

entry.title: Re: Call web service error queryA ENOTFOUND
entry.content:
Yes, here it is in the project I only have three activities

Custom Script

custom code

let input = {
 "CompanyDB": "URGO_TEST",
    "Password": "xxxxxxxxx.",
    "UserName": "layer"
};
 
return {
    method: 'POST',
    url: "https://45.163.29.123:50000/b1s/v1/Login",
     responseType: 'json',
   // ignoreInvalidCookies: true,
    resolveBodyOnly: true,
    json: input,
   headers: {
        'Accept': 'application/json',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    //useWinCa: true,
    resetCookie: true,
   //rejectUnauthorized: false,
   https: { rejectUnauthorized: false },
   requestCert: false
};

 

Call Web Service

Log Message

 

The error is 

Error Detail 1: "Login job started before irpa_core.request.call activity ended. Missing await ?"

{
    "type": "automationActivity",
    "name": "irpa_core.log",
    "label": "irpa_core.log",
    "inputs": [
        "Login job started before irpa_core.request.call activity ended. Missing await ?",
        {
            "key": "Error",
            "value": 1
        },
        null,
        true
    ]
}

Error Detail 2:  "queryA ENOTFOUND 45.163.29.123",

{
    "uid": "3cd6130c-349e-472e-a699-0dc36319efb2",
    "name": "Login",
    "type": "automation",
    "instanceUid": "ec71ec36-11bd-4c0d-8f4f-cccc3ddd663a",
    "packageUid": "b504d3f7-1568-4be2-aa7e-218ce6980df6",
    "packageVersionUid": "b55196a8-62af-4df2-8e64-47ef6df1e7f5",
    "attended": true,
    "duration": 22,
    "status": "Failed",
    "code": "KO",
    "label": "queryA ENOTFOUND 45.163.29.123",
    "parentInstanceUid": "cc6ace3f-b4b5-939c-4507-687b8f0ac6fd",
    "containerInstanceUid": "ec71ec36-11bd-4c0d-8f4f-cccc3ddd663a",
    "line": 2,
    "file": "bundle.js",
    "exception": "irpa_core.error.Error",
    "stack": "Error: queryA ENOTFOUND 45.163.29.123    at Object.b [as cast] (C:\Users\freya\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\fcbf6823-f7e1-44d8-b146-4ce402b60288\dist\bundle.js:2:210766)    at y.start (C:\Users\freya\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\fcbf6823-f7e1-44d8-b146-4ce402b60288\dist\bundle.js:2:290940)    at async Object.p [as startJob] (C:\Users\freya\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\fcbf6823-f7e1-44d8-b146-4ce402b60288\dist\bundle.js:2:284758)"
}

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja ,

We are running custom code analysis for project type S/4 HANA migration in the Fiori app. We have also captured the SUSG data which we are passing during the analysis run. 

My question is, in the scope overview all I can see is the number of objects which got scoped or the packages scoped. But we wanted to get a list of all the objects with objects name which are directly/indirectly(referenced) scoped. Is it possible to get such a list from SUSG data or from the Fiori App straightaway?

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract - long term paid invoices
entry.content:
Hi @CarstenA, 

For Scenario 1, if you don't want to deal with Delta settlement. I would recommend basing the settlement on the Clearing Date rather than the Billing Date to ensure that only paid invoices are considered in settlements.

To achieve this, you may need to create a custom CDS view to retrieve the Clearing Date (AUGDT) for the FI document related to the billing document. The key relationships for this retrieval are:

  • AWTYP = 'VBRK'
  • AWKEY = VBRK.VBELN

In the Settlement Settings of the Condition Contract Type, you can still choose to accrue based on the Billing Date by setting the field COCO_DETERM_DATE to "2 - Billing Date (KOMK-FKDAT)". This ensures that accruals are generated correctly while settlements take payments into account.

For Scenario 2, you are correct that SAP CCM is not designed for contracts to remain open indefinitely (e.g., with an end date of 9999). While it is technically possible, I would not recommend it due to several potential downsides, including:

  • Open accruals never being fully reversed, leading to discrepancies in financial reporting.
  • Performance issues due to excessive data accumulation over time.
  • Line item limitations, depending on how splits are managed within the system.

If your client insists on keeping contracts open for extended periods, it’s crucial to clearly communicate these risks and establish an appropriate monitoring and reconciliation process to maintain accurate financial reporting.

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: update field of interna table from another internal table
entry.content: Where is this myth coming from? LOOP ENDLOOP is not causing performance issue! Constructor expressions are essentially used for filling from scratch one variable (replace its value), not for updating.

entry.title: Re: Where-used-list for many objects
entry.content: We had a similar issue, because we wanted to find out, in preparation to go from ECC 6.0 to Hana, which Z-Objects are still used. So we used the functionalities of SUSG and SCMON to write the trace into the SCMON* tables. We kept in the SUSG we default value of 1 week, to not overload the SCMON tables and created a job to download on a weekly bases the entries for the Z* objects in the tables. So we build a directory of Z-objects. I hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: Gts commodity code transfer to S4
entry.content: Ahh, so you must be on GTSE4H. So, the messages come as standard. Have you checked table BDCP2?

entry.title: Re: Design Patterns in ABAP: Factory and singleton (and the wonderous world of OO-Abap)
entry.content: Well, what do you know. I was looking for a thorough explanation on a few ABAP design patterns just to stumble upon one written by my former teamlead @Jan-WillemK 🙂 Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Receiving 997 functional acknowledgement in SAP with TPM in CPI
entry.content: Checked the setup in TPM V2 and it doesn't appear to offer any mapping of a 997 for transmission into the back-end. One of the main reasons (there are others) that we do not use the TPM framework and have a custom one instead.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM quarterly update (Operations)
entry.content:
Stay up to date with new and enhanced functionality in SAP Cloud ALM. The updates look back at what has been delivered in the previous quarter.

Learn directly from subject matter experts about what's new and have your questions answered!

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: Receiving 997 functional acknowledgement in SAP with...
entry.content: Are you using the XI adapter?

entry.title: Re: Use Date input control in a SAC formula to calculate...
entry.content:
@adriano1973 

The Date Difference calculation only allows Date and Current Date/timestamp as the 2 date parameters. So currently this would not be possible. If starting date was a single date and not repeated for other dimensions you could have handled this in script but assuming starting date is tagged to data itself calculating the datediff betwen each record starting date and input control date selection would not be possible.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Business One - RSP Backup Failed Due to HANA Folder Permission Issue
entry.content:
Hi SAP TechUsers,

I'm facing an issue in SAP Business One where the RSP backup fails due to HANA folder permission issues. Within 2-3 days, the folder permissions need to be restored using chmod 777. After resetting the folder permissions, the backup works properly. However, I need to set the permissions to 777 daily.

Could anyone help me identify the cause of this issue? Additionally, could you provide guidance on how to set the permissions in the daily crontab?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Stay up to date with new and enhanced functionality in SAP Cloud ALM. The updates look back at what has been delivered in the previous quarter.

For slides and recordings click here.

entry.title: Re: OData Service in Service Consumption Model returns http response format text/html
entry.content: How to set up basic authentication within the code?

entry.title: Re: Why does Hybris startup hang when near SessionFixatio...
entry.content: It's rather a workaround than a solution. I reported the problem to the SAP Support and they opened a ticket for their dev team to actually fix this issue. Hopefully this will be resolved in the next version.

entry.title: Re: How to get the total number of data point comments in...
entry.content:
@ramkumar_thayumanavan 

No function or API which returns count of comments so currently this seems to be a limitation.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAC Live Connection Schedule Publication not working
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a SAC Live connection and enabled everyday publication for some reports, It works everyday but suddenly Its not working. The Error message says, "We couldn't authenticate you. Please make sure the credentials provided for connections are valid"

We haven't change anything, even Production system Password also working. Can anyone please suggest why this error came? and where to check? 

Best Regards

Sathiya

entry.title: Re: Delete the whole planning category in SAP S4/HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Dear @JanaHriv,

after aligning with my colleagues, I have to admit that my answer was not precise enough:

The check, if data is available in a specific category, is performed for categories with an Application Type other than Periodic Planning, because the category and the data must be deleted consistently (in accordance with the respective application).

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Direct Network Path Backup in SAP Business One HANA DB Server
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech Users,

I have a question regarding SAP Business One HANA DB Server backup. Is it possible to configure a direct network path backup in RSP Backup, such as \\127.0.0.1\Backup Path? If not, is there any other SAP inbuilt utility that can solve this problem and allow the backup to go directly to a network-located NAS drive?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate Testing Workstream
entry.content:
Hi guys, please I'm doing my certification and I have a doubt in one question about test automation toll for S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. Please, anyone can help me with the correct answer.

The question is:

Which of the following are capabilities of the test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition? Select 2 alternatives.
1) Raises test defects automatically
2) Supports recording of transaction-based processes
3) Supports the creation od custom test automates
4) Captures test results with screenshot

In my opinion the correct answer is options 3 and 4. But I found another forum with the option 2 is correct too.
Please, anyone can help me with this question?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: IPS Delta Read
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have defined the following properties in the Source System (Azure): ips.full.read.force.count=3.

In the Target System (Identity Authentication), I have defined this property: ips.delete.existedbefore.entities=true.

Then, I deleted a group in IAS. I thought that when the READ job is executed 3 times (which would be Delta Read), a Full Read would be triggered on the 4th run, and the deleted group would be re-provisioned from the Source System to the Target System.

However, this is not happening.

What am I doing wrong?

How can I schedule the Delta Read job? I cannot find the ips.delta.read=enebaled parameter in either the Source or the Target system.

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: The Future of Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question towards the use of the Cost-of-Sales ledger (table GLFUNCT). I understand that this is also part of the Compatibility Pack until 31 Dec 2025 - my question is if it is really necessary to find an alternative way to build up reporting based on a P&L by functional area (in other words: rebuild reports, programs,... to read from ACDOCA instead of GLFUNCT)?

We are using GLFUNCT only - so only summarized data, but no line items for ledger 0F. Currently on:
S4HANA ON PREMISE 2022 01 (02/2023) FPS (private cloud - RISE), with an upgrade to 2023 FPS02 planned in Q2/2025.

We are using GLFUNCT data only for reporting purposes (a GR53 report built on library 0M1 and a couple of Z-reports - which are (unfortunately 😉  ) VERY relevant for our month-end closing process).

My understanding during the S/4 implementation project (brown-field approach, from ECC6.0 / classical G/L accounting  to S/4HANA Universal Journal with account-based multiple-GAAP approach, only calculatoric CO-PA) was that GLFUNCT is still populated (which is true, we just had to adapt a few settings, like making sure that KSU5/KSV5 cycle postings are reflected in GLFUNCT correctly - which was not necessary in ECC6.0 since transaction KALC took care of functional area "shifts"), it just won't be further developed by SAP and support for it will stop after 31 Dec. 2025.

I am aware that changing the above mentioned reports to use ACDOCA instead will be necessary at some point in time - I am just afraid that this point in time will already be 31 Dec 2025, since there is already a full project pipeline ahead of us 🙂


Thanks & have a nice day,
Benni

entry.title: Re: How to get the total number of data point comments in...
entry.content:
Thanks @N1kh1l 

Actually I got that in the model preferences there is comment statistics which shows total count of the comments.

There are nearly 1000 comments which has to be transported now from one tenant to another but as we discussed in earlier questions transporting is not possible

So I am really struggling on this comment transport solution

entry.title: Do you know which services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are performed by SAP?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Administering SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Interaction with SAP learning journey.

Overview
After completing this learning journey, the learners will be able to identify which services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are performed by SAP, and which services can only be performed by the customer. Additionally, they will understand the use cases for SAP Service requests including their process flow and how to create Service Requests to interact with SAP, what input SAP requires to accomplish an ordered task, and what output to expect.

Learning objectives
This course will prepare to:

  • Explain the meaning of the Roles and Responsibilities document
  • Classify services in the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Explain the use of SAP Cloud Application Services
  • Illustrate the Service Process Flow using examples.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP HANA DB Server Installation on SUSE Linux
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech Users,

I need to know that, while installing SAP HANA DB SERVER on SUSE Linux, does it install both the application and database server on a single server, or does it only install the database server and the application server remains on a Windows server? Additionally, what web server services does it use on SUSE Linux or any other Linux platform? Does it use Apache, Nginx, or a self-built web server, and where is it located? What is the web service or web server name?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Does anyone know where I can download compass example...
entry.content:
@SemJon 

Looking at the help page it looks like the Best Bike Model. May be you could try the sample planning model in Content network. You might have to adapt a bit.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: how to handle unplanned freight cost
entry.content: Scenario: in our system, user created PR, PR go through the approval flow, after PR released, PR will be converted to PO, supplier delivered products and sent the invoice which included the freight cost, this freight cost was not included in PO. Question: how do we handle the unplanned freight cost? we don't want to PO go through the approval flow again, and slow down the invoice posting?

entry.title: Re: Can we schedule a standalone bonus calculation recurr...
entry.content:
Hi 

Yes, you can schedule a standalone recurring job for Set Bonus Calculation in VarPay. Use the Set Bonus Calculation job in SuccessFactors Compensation to trigger bonus calculations for all employees, regardless of EC record changes. Schedule it separately from the ECT History Processor job at your preferred frequency. Ensure it runs after any updates to business goals, weights, or bonus plans to reflect the latest changes. Configure the job in Provisioning or Admin Center under Compensation Home > Scheduled Jobs.

entry.title: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content:
Hello community


I'm new here and need some help. Is there a way to make a calculated measure where its multiply the leafs members in a hierarchy? I didn't find any product function to use. Should i make a data action for it?

entry.title: How to Switch from GET to POST Call in SAPUI5 Analytical Table with Node.js Backend?
entry.content:
Hello SCN community,

I am working with an SAPUI5 Analytical Table bound to a Node.js backend service using analytical binding, and everything is working as expected with the default GET request. However, I now need to change the default behavior to use a POST request instead of GET in order to send additional data along with the request.

Current Setup:

var oModel = this.getView().getModel("analyticalModel");
oModel._request = odataModelRequest(oModel._request, this._waitODataLongReq.bind(this));

var oBindingParams = {
entitySet: "dynamicEntity",
useAnalyticalBinding: true,
initiallyVisibleFields: '',
useBatchRequests: false,
provideGrandTotals: true,
hasTotaledMeasures: true,
provideTotalResultSize: true,
reloadSingleUnitMeasures: true,
sOperationMode: sap.ui.model.odata.OperationMode.Server,
autoExpandMode: 'Sequential',
custom: { // Adding custom query parameters here
params: sParams
}
};

let fecBindcontext = {
model: oModel,
path: "/dynamicEntity"
};

var spath = `/storage/sys/${this._UUID}/se11/${this.tables}/dynamicEntity`;
var oTableBinding = new sap.ui.model.analytics.AnalyticalBinding(oModel, spath, fecBindcontext, [], [], oBindingParams);
oTable.setModel(oModel);

 

My Questions:

  1. Does the SAPUI5 Analytical Table support POST requests?
  2. If yes, could someone guide me on how to implement this?
    • Specifically, as it is a direct binding, I need to know how to modify the request type and how to pass the data (which is in another tab) in the body of the POST request to fetch the data in the View Output tab

      Any help or examples would be greatly appreciated!

      Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Classes not showing in date order
entry.content:
Hi 

The class sorting in SAP SuccessFactors LMS may not always default to chronological order.
The order is typically influenced by system sorting logic, which can be based on Class ID, Offering ID, or a backend sorting mechanism.

To fix this, check these settings in LMS Admin:
1. Admin Center > Learning Administration > System Configuration > LMS_ADMIN – Look for sorting-related settings.
2. Class List View Configuration – Ensure sorting is set to Start Date (ascending or descending).
3. Reports & Queries – If using a custom report, verify the sorting logic in the query.

If sorting still appears incorrect, consider logging a case with SAP Support for further investigation.

entry.title: Re: Use Date input control in a SAC formula to calculate...
entry.content: Hello N1kh1l, apart date input control, is possible in a formula, to calculate a date difference, asking to the user to fill a date on the webpage to use as a "To date" in the formula? That is, is not possible any way to calculate a date difference having only a starting date, and getting the "To date" somewhere where the user can set it? Thank you

entry.title: WTB - Permission for Assignmenthistory
entry.content:
Hi,

 

in the dispatching board for me as an administrator there is the possibility to see historical changes of an assignments by click on the assignment.

I couldn"t find the correct permission for this, please give me a short reply so I find it.

Best regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: how to handle unplanned freight cost
entry.content:
Hello,

Did you look for the creation of a subsequent debit? This can be a solution.

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning HUB
entry.content:

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: HANA SQL QUERY TO FETCH NESTED JSON OBJECTS
entry.content:
hi ,

I have a table machine_group with values as shown.

 

Now using a query as show below

select * from machine_group for json

the values fetched as below 

[
  {"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL1"},{"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL2"},{"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL3"},
  {"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL5"},{"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL6"},{"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL7"}
 ]

But am looking to fetch the output in nested json format as shown below.

 
[
{
"COLA":"A",
"COLB":[ {"COLB":"VAL1"}, {"COLB":"VAL2"}, {"COLB":"VAL3"}]
},
{
"COLA":"B",
"COLB":[ {"COLB":"VAL4"}, {"COLB":"VAL5"}, {"COLB":"VAL6"}]
}
]

 

Please help me. 

Regards

Govardan

entry.title: Re: Business Process Integration certificate
entry.content:
Hi @ManojRaju
this looks like a new question.
Can you please post it in our SAP Learning Q&A

This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses
Would you mind to repost it ?

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: CCM - Statistical price condition in delta accruals
entry.content:
Hi 

Your issue likely stems from the missing Accrual flag in RED1 and incorrect account determination. Try these steps:
1. Ensure RED1 is correctly mapped to REA1 in the pricing procedure and flagged as Accruals.
2. Activate the accrual flag for RED1 in V/06 (Condition Type Configuration) to ensure it behaves like REA1.
3. Verify the account determination settings in T-code OBYC (Transaction Key for Accruals) to ensure accrual-related accounts are triggered.
4. Check the posting configuration in 0S42, ensuring Posting Statistical Condition to Accounting is correctly set.
5. If the FI document does not generate, run ACDOCA checks to confirm statistical postings are processed.
6. Test with a new pricing procedure if issues persist, ensuring statistical conditions are correctly assigned.

entry.title: Re: PCR for OT calculations
entry.content:
I don't think you need separate time types which puts the burden back on the employee or end user recording the time. Try using VARSTDAYTY to determine if it is a scheduled or unscheduled day. Then you can use FILLP to change the valuation type.

I hope this helps. 

Cindy

entry.title: Re: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi @priscilla_barbosa82 

You can use the lookup function in the model calculated measures to make use of the leaf nodes in hierarchy.

You can also have the account dimension where Formula column is readily available for it.

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: Re: Memories of the Newtown Square Campus
entry.content:
@jerryjanda Thank you for your share and compassion for the history of the Lyondell buildings, SAP Campus including NSQ1 and NSQ2.   The grounds, buildings and expansion in NSQ is truly bittersweet.   Yes, the geese are also a hazard.  

I remember my first snowfall at NSQ.  I live in NJ and drove the treacherous winding roads of Bishop Hallow to NSQ (prior to the 1/2 being added).   The snow was falling and the landscape untouched.   I remember thinking to myself this is my vision of Robert Frost's poem "Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening".

entry.title: Re: Import existing BPM source code project NWDS
entry.content:
Hello ;

You can perform the following steps via the NWDS application.
Create a new SC under the Development Infrastructure view

 

 

 

The SC you created will look like this:

 

Delete the resources in SC by selecting them from the root section

In the final case, your SC will appear empty as below.

 

 

Extract your own source codes to a folder and import these resources to nwds from the import section below.

 

 

 

When you complete the above operations and click the finish button, the following screen will open.

After selecting Yes, you can add your source files under the SC you created before.

 


 

In the last case, you can see your own source files under SC. After the build process, your bpm will be ready under the process development view.

Once the above process is completed, switch to the Process development view.

 

Your BPM is ready,

Regards

Hasan

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content: Pessoal, talvez não seja a melhor solução, mas resolveu meu problema... Eu simplesmente desinstalei tudo do meu eclipse, incluindo o workspace e instalei outro IDE. Estava usando o que está destacado em vermelho na versão 2024-09 quando deu pau. Instalei o que está em verde na mesma versão 2024-09 e deu boa. Espero que de alguma forma ajude.

entry.title: Re: SAP Reference Architecture Framework (SEAF) & RBA
entry.content:
Hi Lorimer1,

thanks a lot for your post. I am aware that the current delay in updating the SAP Reference Architecture in Signavio compared to LeanIX causes issues for you. We are looking for possibilities to tackle this problem.

If you want to engage with us on the SAP Reference Architecture and its representation in LeanIX and Signavio please consider to subscribe to the customer engagement initiative "SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX". This will be published on influence.sap.com and the registration is planned to be open from Feb 24th to March 28th. Please feel also free to reach out directly to me under martin.effenberger@sap.com

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content: Thanks for the answer @ramkumar_thayumanavan.. Would you help me on the formula on the calculated measure, please? I've tried but it didn't work. I need the product result in each parent node

entry.title: Re: Can we schedule a standalone bonus calculation recurr...
entry.content: Sorry, I should have clarified that I have already tried to do so in Provisioning. Also, there is no way to create a scheduled job in Compensation Home, only monitor any jobs that are running or have run.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content: Please find this image . This was the calculated measure created on the model

entry.title: SAP version mismatch after S/4 conversion
entry.content:
Dear Support

After conversion system to S/4, from SAP version ERP EHP 6 to SAP version S/4 2023,gGoing to the
Installed Product Version
I see the two versions at the same time (see attachment)

Could you help me understand?
If so, is it possible to delete the old entry?

entry.title: SAC Composites - Retrieve/Update Interface Properties at script level
entry.content:
Is there anyway to access the properties you create for a composite at script level?

I'm only seeing events we've defined when using COMPOSITE_1.x, and we cant update the properties within the event scripts themselves when defining the composite. Would make more sense to update the properties that are bindable.

entry.title: Re: What’s New Preview Available for SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2025
entry.content:
@Devan_Gee is it possible to get a little more detail on below item, because we cannot locate this functionality on our preview fast-track tenant, and the roadmap link mentioned seems to refer to another kind of job monitor?
"You can now monitor the jobs created while transporting the content. All jobs related to transport operations (such as Export, Import, Download, and Upload) are listed in the Monitor page"
